mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience with integrated Vultr features.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Use the Vultr CLI to create a new Kubernetes cluster. Replace `<region>` and `<plan>` with your desired values.
```bash
vultr-cli k8s create --region <region> --plan <plan> --k8s-version 1.30
```

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
After the cluster is created, configure kubectl to connect to your new cluster. Replace `<cluster-id>` with your actual cluster ID.
```bash
vultr-cli k8s config <cluster-id> --output kubeconfig.yaml
export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kubeconfig.yaml
kubectl cluster-info
```

steps.title: Install Vultr Cloud Controller Manager
steps.description:
Download and apply the necessary YAML files for the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager. Ensure you replace `<region-id>` and `<api-key>` with your actual Vultr region ID and API key.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/releases/secret.yml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/releases/v0.16.0.yml
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager pods to ensure they are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep vultr-cloud-controller-manager
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling for your cluster. Ensure the metrics server is installed and then configure the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA).
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml
kubectl autoscale deployment <deployment-name> --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
Use the Vultr CLI to delete the Kubernetes cluster. Replace `<cluster-id>` with your actual cluster ID.
```bash
vultr-cli k8s delete <cluster-id>
```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure all resources related to the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager are removed.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/releases/v0.16.0.yml
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/releases/secret.yml
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration. Then, apply the new version YAML. Replace `v0.16.0` with the new version if necessary.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/releases/v0.16.0.yml
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager pods are not starting, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system
``` Ensure that your API key and region ID are correctly set in the secret.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl Not Connecting
troubleshooting.description:
If kubectl cannot connect to the cluster, verify your kubeconfig file.
```bash
cat $KUBECONFIG
``` Make sure it points to the correct cluster and that the credentials are valid.

troubleshooting.title: LoadBalancer Not Provisioned
troubleshooting.description:
If a LoadBalancer service is not being provisioned, ensure that the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager is running and check the service configuration.
```bash
kubectl describe service <service-name>
``` Look for events indicating provisioning issues.

versionNotes.changes: Improved integration with Vultr features and enhanced stability.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecated features in this version.

resolution.summary: A successful setup will have a running Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager installed and configured. You should be able to deploy applications and utilize Vultr's cloud features seamlessly.
codeSnippets:
vultr-cli k8s config <cluster-id> --output kubeconfig.yaml
export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kubeconfig.yaml
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep vultr-cloud-controller-manager

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Vultr CLI installed and configured with your API key.
